STATUS: INVALID — this file documents an apples-to-oranges comparison
reason_invalid: The inline Potts analysis compared a DIFFERENT mathematical object to PCA PC1 than Exp 60 Parts A/C.
Parts A/C compare: mean-tangent direction J*v1 (how mu moves when theta moves along stiffest direction).
Potts inline compared: whitened direction Sigma^{-1}*d(mu)/dt or PCA of within-covariance.
These are different objects. When the SAME comparison (J*v1 vs PC1) is applied to Potts, cos ~ 0.999.
Therefore the 'cos = 0.211 smoking gun' was an artifact of inconsistent comparison, not a real signal.

original_claims_retracted.cos_fisher_pca_0.211: RETRACTED — based on wrong comparison object
original_claims_retracted.diagnostic_gate: RETRACTED — not supported by consistent cross-system data

what_to_do_next: Re-run Potts using estimate_fisher_matrix from 60_fisher_information.py with J @ eigenvectors[:,0] comparison, matching Parts A/C exactly.
